flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: Case 6b + regression pattern (a) — Stage 0.5 reject is conjecture-level: the flagship generic selector-range Conjecture 1 is not proved and the unqualified one-dimensional claim is explicitly false; the only derivations achieved sit at subfield tier and Proposition 4's witness has a structural rank-stability defect (A=0 duplicate rows), so re-deriving on the same .tex cannot meet the flagship novelty floor — pivot to a new angle.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp identification phase transition for the average treatment effect via proximal causal inference (negative controls) when standard proxy completeness fails. Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika) and Cui, Pu, Shi, Miao, Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, JASA) established exact point identification of the ATE under unmeasured confounding via the outcome bridge function h(W,A,X) solving E[Y - h(W,A,X) | Z,A,X]=0, provided the completeness condition that g -> E[g(U) | Z,A,X] is injective on a suitable function class. Recent work (Tchetgen Tchetgen, Ying, Cui, Shi, Miao 2024; Kompa, Sturma, Imbens 2024 NBER w34550) studies failure modes when completeness is only approximately satisfied. The flagship question: parametrize completeness by the smallest singular value sigma_min of the projection operator g -> E[g(U) | Z,A,X] restricted to bridge-function residuals, and characterize a sharp phase transition: when sigma_min > sigma_star (a critical threshold computable from the observable proxy distribution and outcome regression), the ATE is exactly point-identified by the proximal IPW/OR estimator; when 0 < sigma_min <= sigma_star, the ATE is identified by a sharp non-trivial interval whose width scales as O(1/sigma_min); when sigma_min = 0 the proximal identification collapses entirely to a Manski-trivial bound. The kernel claim is a sharp closed-form characterization of sigma_star as the smallest singular value of an explicit observable kernel, with strict point/interval/Manski phase boundaries. Recovers Miao-Geng-Tchetgen point identification in the high-completeness regime and provides the first sharp proximal phase transition theorem with computable threshold.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains pid_mediation_proxy, eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator, and q1_spectral_phase_transition. Those artifacts concern proxy-null sharpness warnings, mediator-coupling proxy envelopes, and panel/FWL spectral thresholds; none proposes a finite proximal OR/DR-vs-minimax native-equivalence frontier comparing identified sets, selected ATE maps, and local first-order/efficiency-bound objects.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023SPL and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT for nonunique/weak bridges with strongly identified functionals, BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023MinimaxIV for penalized minimax convergence without uniqueness or closedness, CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024JASA for proximal OR/IPW/DR, and KallusMaoUehara2021MinimaxNC for minimax negative-control learning. The revised ε-gap is a computable finite proximal diagnostic for when OR/DR and profiled minimax native objectives share the same singleton identified set and, locally on a zero-threshold fixed-rank stratum, the same first-order/efficiency-bound object; the cited work does not give that native-equivalence frontier."}
seed_list: Prior work ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023SPL has established that bridge nonuniqueness can coexist with identified counterfactual means. Our seed conjectures a sharper three-phase ATE support classification, because the ATE loading may annihilate some bridge-null directions while bounded attainable null directions still generate exact interval and Manski regimes.

Prior work MiaoGengTchetgen2018Biometrika would imply point identification once proxy rank/completeness holds. Our seed conjectures that rank failure does not imply total collapse, because the finite bridge-null space can be nonzero but invisible to the ATE loading.

Prior work GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPI establishes proxy-based bounds without completeness. Our seed conjectures those bounds have a finite spectral support-function collapse criterion, because null-space orientation, not proxy count alone, decides whether the interval is point, nontrivial, or Manski-trivial.

Prior work CanaySantosShaikh2013Econometrica would warn that completeness diagnostics are not testable in unrestricted nonparametric models. Our seed conjectures a finite-support exception and an unrestricted impossibility frontier, because observability of singular directions depends on whether the proxy operator is an identified matrix or an infinite-dimensional latent operator.

Prior work CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024JASA establishes efficient proximal OR/IPW/DR estimators under bridge conditions. Our seed conjectures that the same workflow has a deterministic identification-radius diagnostic when completeness fails, because DR orthogonality cannot remove variation along causal bridge-null directions.

Prior work BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT studies strongly identified functionals of weakly identified nuisance functions. Our seed conjectures the complementary sharp weak-functional set for proximal ATEs, because failure of strong identification should produce an exact support interval rather than only slower inference.

Prior work Santos2011JoE shows continuous linear functionals can be recovered even when the NPIV function is not identified. Our seed conjectures a proximal finite-state adjoint-range criterion with bounded-null interval fallback, because negative-control bridge fibers add box-constrained causal attainability absent from generic NPIV.

Prior work Andrews2017JoECompleteness provides L2-complete and incomplete distribution examples. Our seed conjectures that two proxy laws with the same smallest singular value can have different ATE identified widths, because the estimand loading can be orthogonal or aligned with the weak singular directions.

Prior work DarollesFanFlorensRenault2011Econometrica treats ill-posed inverse equations through regularization. Our seed conjectures that two admissible regularizers can select different proximal ATE limits inside the same sharp interval, because regularization resolves bridge nonuniqueness without adding identifying information.

Prior work ShiMiaoNelsonTchetgen2020JRSSB develops double-negative-control adjustment with categorical latent structure. Our seed conjectures an exact finite transition-matrix LP for rank-deficient negative controls, because categorical proxy matrices expose bridge-null boxes that continuous completeness assumptions hide.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023SPL has established that bridge nonuniqueness can coexist with identified counterfactual means. Our seed conjectures a sharper three-phase ATE support classification, because the ATE loading may annihilate some bridge-null directions while bounded attainable null directions still generate exact interval and Manski regimes.

anchor_paper.author: Jeffrey Zhang, Wei Li, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Shows bridge-equation solutions may be nonunique and develops inference for a counterfactual mean without imposing uniqueness assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; it asks for the sharp complement of functional identification, not a weaker assumption.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT because strongly identified functionals are already characterized by an adjoint condition.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: COLT gives inference conditions for point functionals, not the finite sharp ATE identified interval and Manski boundary when the condition fails.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MiaoGengTchetgen2018Biometrika would imply point identification once proxy rank/completeness holds. Our seed conjectures that rank failure does not imply total collapse, because the finite bridge-null space can be nonzero but invisible to the ATE loading.

anchor_paper.author: Wang Miao, Zhi Geng, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Establishes nonparametric point identification with proxy variables under rank/completeness-type conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the seed exposes harmless rank failure versus causal rank failure, a phase distinction absent from the point-ID theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MiaoGengTchetgen2018Biometrika because completeness is exactly the rank boundary.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the Biometrika result treats sufficient point identification and does not classify rank-deficient ATE identified sets.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPI establishes proxy-based bounds without completeness. Our seed conjectures those bounds have a finite spectral support-function collapse criterion, because null-space orientation, not proxy count alone, decides whether the interval is point, nontrivial, or Manski-trivial.

anchor_paper.author: AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Motivates partial identification because completeness is not empirically testable and derives proxy-based causal-effect bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not a CATE/ATT/sign variant; it sharpens the geometry of published proxy bounds by adding collapse and triviality boundaries.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPI because their bounds already describe noncompleteness.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the paper gives proxy bounds but not a singular-subspace phase diagram or finite realization theorem for bridge-null fibers.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CanaySantosShaikh2013Econometrica would warn that completeness diagnostics are not testable in unrestricted nonparametric models. Our seed conjectures a finite-support exception and an unrestricted impossibility frontier, because observability of singular directions depends on whether the proxy operator is an identified matrix or an infinite-dimensional latent operator.

anchor_paper.author: Ivan A. Canay, Andres Santos, Azeem M. Shaikh
anchor_paper.claim: Shows no nontrivial tests exist for certain completeness and identification hypotheses in nonparametric endogenous models.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility pattern is not blocked because it targets the named published non-testability theorem and would state a finite-dimensional exception plus unrestricted no-go class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from CanaySantosShaikh2013Econometrica because they already prove non-testability.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: they do not analyze finite observed negative-control matrices or a three-phase proximal ATE classifier.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024JASA establishes efficient proximal OR/IPW/DR estimators under bridge conditions. Our seed conjectures that the same workflow has a deterministic identification-radius diagnostic when completeness fails, because DR orthogonality cannot remove variation along causal bridge-null directions.

anchor_paper.author: Yifan Cui, Hongming Pu, Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Develops proximal ATE efficiency and doubly robust estimation under bridge/completeness conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not an estimator extension; it adds an identification-radius obstruction to a named DR workflow.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from semiparametric proximal theory because nonidentification just means estimators are invalid.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: invalidity is qualitative; the seed asks for the exact finite sharp interval that remains after bridge failure.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT studies strongly identified functionals of weakly identified nuisance functions. Our seed conjectures the complementary sharp weak-functional set for proximal ATEs, because failure of strong identification should produce an exact support interval rather than only slower inference.

anchor_paper.author: Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Whitney Newey, Vasilis Syrgkanis, Masatoshi Uehara
anchor_paper.claim: Studies inference on continuous linear functionals when nuisance functions are weakly identified or have multiple solutions, including proximal examples.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: it is the partial-ID complement of a named strong-functional theorem, with a new support interval rather than a routine assumption weakening.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the COLT adjoint-range condition because failure implies nonidentification.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: failure of point identification does not give sharp lower and upper causal-effect bounds or Manski boundaries.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Santos2011JoE shows continuous linear functionals can be recovered even when the NPIV function is not identified. Our seed conjectures a proximal finite-state adjoint-range criterion with bounded-null interval fallback, because negative-control bridge fibers add box-constrained causal attainability absent from generic NPIV.

anchor_paper.author: Andres Santos
anchor_paper.claim: Develops recovery of continuous linear functionals in NPIV problems, separating functional identification from full structural-function identification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: it transfers the adjoint insight only after adding proximal causal attainability and sharp interval fallback.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Santos2011JoE because the ATE is just a linear functional.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Santos does not treat negative-control causal models, bounded bridge fibers, or sharp ATE partial identification.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Andrews2017JoECompleteness provides L2-complete and incomplete distribution examples. Our seed conjectures that two proxy laws with the same smallest singular value can have different ATE identified widths, because the estimand loading can be orthogonal or aligned with the weak singular directions.

anchor_paper.author: Donald W. K. Andrews
anchor_paper.claim: Provides L2-complete and boundedly-complete distribution examples, but not sharp identified sets across rank-deficient regimes.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: it challenges scalar singular-value sufficiency with loading orientation, a phase phenomenon.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from singular-vector algebra in any inverse problem textbook.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly known algebraically, but not tied to proximal ATE identified sets and finite causal realization.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DarollesFanFlorensRenault2011Econometrica treats ill-posed inverse equations through regularization. Our seed conjectures that two admissible regularizers can select different proximal ATE limits inside the same sharp interval, because regularization resolves bridge nonuniqueness without adding identifying information.

anchor_paper.author: Serge Darolles, Yanqin Fan, Jean-Pierre Florens, Eric Renault
anchor_paper.claim: Treats nonparametric instrumental regression as an ill-posed inverse problem and uses Tikhonov regularization for estimation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample pattern not blocked because it targets named regularized inverse-problem workflows and would promote to an open class of proximal laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from regularization theory because pseudoinverses depend on penalties.
seed_details.rebuttal: The penalty-dependence is known for functions, but not for causal ATE values relative to a sharp proximal identified set.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ShiMiaoNelsonTchetgen2020JRSSB develops double-negative-control adjustment with categorical latent structure. Our seed conjectures an exact finite transition-matrix LP for rank-deficient negative controls, because categorical proxy matrices expose bridge-null boxes that continuous completeness assumptions hide.

anchor_paper.author: Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Jennifer C. Nelson, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B
anchor_paper.claim: Develops double-negative-control adjustment with categorical latent-confounding structure, highlighting finite-rank proxy restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not blocked: finite categorical rank deficiency creates explicit LP geometry and regime boundaries, not a routine discrete special case.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from finite linear programming once variables are categorical.
seed_details.rebuttal: The LP is routine; the nontrivial part is the sharp causal realization and point/interval/Manski classification.

proposed_from.literature_map: 1. Core proximal point identification: Miao-Geng-Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika) gives proxy-based nonparametric ATE identification under rank/completeness; Cui-Pu-Shi-Miao-Tchetgen (2024, JASA) adds semiparametric efficiency and DR estimators. 2. Proximal overview: Tchetgen Tchetgen-Ying-Cui-Shi-Miao (2024, Statistical Science) frames PCI as replacing exact measured confounding with proxy bridge assumptions. 3. Completeness fragility: Canay-Santos-Shaikh (2013, Econometrica) shows nontrivial tests for completeness/identification can be impossible. 4. Partial identification anchor: Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen (2023) explicitly drops completeness and derives proxy-based causal-effect bounds. 5. NPIV partial identification: Santos (2012, Econometrica) gives inference under partial identification; Santos (2011, JoE) and Severini-Tripathi (2012, JoE) address recoverable linear functionals. 6. Weakly identified functions: Bennett-Kallus-Mao-Newey-Syrgkanis-Uehara (2023) studies strongly identified functionals of weakly identified nuisance functions, including proximal examples. 7. Nonunique bridges: Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen (2023, Statistics & Probability Letters) handles proximal inference without uniqueness assumptions. 8. Ill-posed inverse-problem econometrics: Darolles-Fan-Florens-Renault (2011, Econometrica), Florens-Johannes-Van Bellegem (2011, Econometric Theory), and Horowitz (2014, Annual Review of Economics) supply spectral/regularization background. 9. Completeness conditions: Andrews (2017, Journal of Econometrics) and Hu-Shiu (2018, Econometric Theory) give sufficient examples/conditions, but not sharp failure-region ATE sets. 10. Many/panel proxies: Deaner (2018, Proxy Controls and Panel Data) and Deaner (2021, Many Proxy Controls) connect econometric proxy controls to rank and low-dimensional latent structure. 11. General partial-identification perspective: Kline-Tamer (2023, Annual Review of Economics) and Molinari (2020, Handbook/arXiv) motivate identified-set characterization rather than forced point identification. 12. Weak-IV analogy: Andrews-Stock-Sun (2019, Annual Review of Economics) suggests the right econometric template for weak-proxy robust inference. 13. Search note inherited from Stage -1.1: the named Kompa-Sturma-Imbens NBER w34550 proximal paper was not verified; current NBER/search results identify w34550 as an unrelated DID/event-study survey.

banked_reason: Re-review under widened flagship rubric — Stage 0.5 rejected original derivation, intervention pivoted to fresh angle 2. Angle 2 cycled v0/v2/v3/v4/v5=REVISE@field, v1=REVISE@flagship (single hit), cap exhausted → Stage -0.5 NO-PASS@flagship. Proximal-completeness kernel briefly tagged flagship under axis (c) extension framing but couldn't hold it across revisions. Math sound at field tier.
